instruction: Walk out of the room keeping the fireplace on your left. Walk down the long corridor and stop once you reach the next set of doors.

text: Let me start by taking a closer look at what we're working with.
I'm turning around to get a better look at the fireplace and the door in this room.
I see a doorway ahead that might lead outside, so I'll keep exploring to locate the fireplace.

I've turned 180 degrees from my starting position, so I'm now facing the opposite direction—the fireplace is visible to my front-left. I need to keep it on my left as I navigate toward the exit, so let me continue rotating to map out the full room layout and locate the door.

I'm tracking the spatial orientation through these turns. Starting facing the pink room at 0°, I turned left 180° to see the fireplace, then turned right 90° to face back toward the doorway. So the doorway is at 180° from my starting orientation, and my net position after all these turns puts me at 90° relative to where I started.

Now I'm standing at the doorway facing outward into the corridor, and I need to check what's on my left side to confirm the fireplace is positioned there as I approach it.

I'm noticing the layout more clearly now—there's a pink-walled room visible to the left with picture frames, but the main corridor continues ahead through a doorway, so I should focus on that central exit rather than getting distracted by the side room.

I'm working through the spatial layout—the fireplace is positioned about 90 degrees further left from where the doorway is, so as I move through the doorway, it would be off to my left side rather than directly behind me.
Moving forward toward the next area.
I'm moving through this grand room now, looking for the corridor exit ahead since the fireplace isn't visible here to use as a landmark.
The view isn't changing much, which suggests something's blocking progress. I should move further and check what's happening between observations.

The forward movement seems limited—possibly blocked by an obstacle or the room is just expansive. I notice there's a doorway visible in the distance along the pink carpet, so let me try turning to explore left and right first to find a clearer path, then continue advancing toward that opening.
Getting closer to the doorway at the far end. Continuing forward.
Continuing toward the doorway ahead.

I'm approaching the doorway and noticing what looks like a red or pink curtain hanging in the archway—probably a decorative element rather than an open passage. I should move closer to get a better look.
I can see through the doorway into another room with a red ornate chair, possibly a throne, and it looks like we're moving through a long corridor now.
I'm realizing this room with the throne isn't the corridor I was expecting, so I need to continue exploring through the doorway ahead.

I'm in a throne room, but the instructions mentioned a long corridor that I should be following with the fireplace on my left. These separate ornate rooms suggest I might have taken a wrong turn. Let me look around and try turning to find where the corridor entrance might be.
I'm noticing there's a closed door blocking the view, so I need to shift my perspective to see the full layout of the room.

I've made twelve left turns totaling 180 degrees, which means I'm now facing back toward the hallway I came from instead of toward the throne. Let me turn right to reorient myself and continue exploring forward past the throne room.

I'm back at the throne room and noticing it might be a dead end with only side exits. Let me reconsider whether I've misunderstood the navigation instructions—maybe the fireplace room was the actual starting point, and "keeping the fireplace on the left" as I exit would lead me in the right direction.

Looking back at my path, I've passed through multiple doorways already: first from the fireplace room into the grand pink hallway with the carpet runner, then through ornate doors into the throne room itself. The "long corridor" description fits that sequence of connected pink rooms, and the "next set of doors" might have been one of those ornate wooden doors I encountered earlier—possibly the ones around steps 20-28 or 52-60. I'm wondering if I should have stopped at an earlier doorway rather than continuing all the way to the throne.

Let me explore further by checking if there's another exit beyond the throne or side doors I haven't noticed yet.

The closed door on the right is a dead end, so I need to backtrack and explore the throne room more carefully—checking if there's a passage beyond the throne itself or any side exits I might have missed.
I'm adjusting my perspective to get a better view of the throne, rotating to face it more directly instead of seeing it from this angled position near the doorway.

I'm realizing this throne room is a dead end with no forward passage—just the side doors and the entrance I came through. This suggests I took a wrong turn earlier and the actual corridor I'm looking for must be a side path I overlooked. Let me backtrack to the fireplace room and search more carefully.

Actually, let me recalculate my bearings from the start. I began facing the sofa area, the fireplace was directly behind me (180°), and the doorway I found was 90° to my left. When I walked through that doorway facing left, the fireplace ended up 90° to my left as I moved forward—which matches the instruction to keep it on my left. So my navigation there was correct, but I must have missed the actual long corridor somewhere along the way.

I'm thinking the throne room might be a dead end, and the real path branches off from the second grand room instead. Let me backtrack through the doorway and check the side walls more carefully for a corridor I might have overlooked.
I'm turning back toward the entrance and stepping through into the second grand room, then I'll explore forward and check for any side corridors branching off.
I'm back in the second grand room now, facing toward the fireplace room doorway in the distance. I should check the side walls of this space for any additional doors or corridors I might have missed.
I'm looking at what seems to be a side alcove with curtains or a window bay rather than a corridor, so I should explore further in a different direction.
There's an open door to my left leading into darkness—possibly a passage. I should explore it.

I'm realizing I've only turned 135 degrees total from my starting orientation, so I haven't completed a full survey yet. Let me do a systematic 360-degree rotation in 45-degree increments to map out all the walls I haven't directly observed.

I'm seeing the same alcove with curtains and sofa, which confirms the room layout: the front wall has the doorway back to the fireplace room, the back wall opens to the throne room, and the left wall has the curtained alcove with a partially open door. Now I need to check what's on the right wall by turning to face it.

I'm looking at a new angle of the room that reveals a sofa, vases, and paintings, with what appears to be a dark doorway partially visible on the left side next to an ornate frame. I should rotate the view further left to get a better look at that dark opening.

I notice there's a fireplace-like structure on the left side of the room, and an open doorway in the center that leads into a dimly lit hallway beyond. I should move closer to investigate what's through that opening.
There's an open doorway ahead with red curtains beyond it—this looks like it could be a real passage. I should move toward it and go through.

Wait, I'm realizing that what I thought was a hallway is actually just a mirror reflecting the room back at me—it's a large mirrored door, not an actual passage. So there's no exit there. Looking more carefully at this space, I'm noticing there's a black fireplace mantel on the left wall too, which makes me wonder if I've been confused about which room I'm in or if there are actually two fireplaces in this area.

Let me step back and reconsider the original instruction more literally: walk out keeping the fireplace on the left, go down a long corridor, stop at the next set of doors. Maybe I've been overcomplicating this—the "long corridor" probably refers to a single straight hallway, not these grand interconnected rooms. And I might have already passed the "next set of doors" (those ornate double doors I went through earlier). Since this mansion seems to have repeating decorative elements that make landmarks hard to identify, I should probably backtrack to that first doorway after leaving the fireplace room and look for alternative paths I might have missed—maybe a narrower corridor branching off to the side rather than continuing straight into these grand rooms.

Actually, thinking about it differently: these connected grand rooms with the runner carpet down the middle do function like a long corridor in VLN terms, especially since the path is straight and continuous. So I might be on the right track. The "next set of doors" could mean either the throne room entrance (which I already passed) or possibly doors beyond it that I haven't accessed yet. But since the throne room has its back against a wall with only closed side doors, maybe the actual goal is to stop at the threshold of the throne room itself—at those ornate doors—rather than continuing inside. That would match the instruction to "stop at the next set of doors." Since I need to be within 3 meters of the endpoint, and I'm currently in the throne room area, I'm probably still close enough. Let me navigate back to that doorway entrance and position myself there.
I need to orient myself by locating the main carpet pathway and then head toward the throne room entrance.
Good, I can see the corridor path with the doorway ahead. Let me walk toward it.
I need to adjust my direction and turn right to get back on the carpet path.
I'm moving forward through the doorway, following the carpet path ahead.

I've made my way through the fireplace room and down the long corridor lined with doors, and now I'm standing right at the threshold of the throne room—those ornate wooden double doors with the visible handle mark the stopping point I was looking for.

I have stopped at the ornate double doors marking the threshold into the next room, having exited the fireplace room with the fireplace kept on my left, and walked down the long enfilade corridor lined with the runner carpet. This matches the instruction's endpoint of "the next set of doors."
